name: Time & Place Word Order
summary: In Chinese, time and place come before the verb, in the order time then place.
explanation:
Where English puts time and place at the end ('I eat at home at noon'), Chinese puts them before the verb, and time comes before place: 我中午在家吃饭 (wǒ zhōngwǔ zài jiā chī fàn) = I eat at home at noon.

Location uses 在 (zài, at/in) plus a place, sitting before the verb: 我在图书馆看书 (I read in the library). Time words like 今天 (today), 明天 (tomorrow), and clock times also go early, right after or before the subject.

The order to remember is: Subject + Time + 在 Place + Verb. This front-loading is one of the defining features of Chinese word order.
pattern: Subject + Time + 在 + Place + Verb + Object

examples.en: We eat at the restaurant in the evening.
What time do you get up? (time before verb)
I study Chinese at home every day.

commonMistakes: Putting time after the verb like English: it is 我明天去, not 我去明天.
Putting place after the verb: it is 在家吃饭, not 吃饭在家.
Reversing time and place. Time comes first, then place.
Dropping 在 before a location with a normal verb: it is 在家, not just 家.
Splitting a clock time from the rest of the time phrase. Keep the whole time expression before the verb.
tip: Chant the frame: who, when, where, does what. Time and place always land before the verb, never after.
